meta.status_update: PENDING predictions updated with real-time news velocity analysis
meta.analysis_notes: Top heating-up: AI/Robots (92%), Deepfakes (90%), Glacier Melt (88%), Crypto (87%), Smart Homes (83%)

predictions.title: Three-Eyed Fish
predictions.prediction: Blinky, a three-eyed mutant fish, lives near Springfield's nuclear plant.
predictions.real_event: A three-eyed fish was caught near a nuclear power plant in Argentina.
predictions.episode_name: Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish
predictions.desc: In 'Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish', Bart catches Blinky — a three-eyed mutant fish living downstream from the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant — and the story becomes a political scandal for Mr. Burns. In 2011, fishermen near the Embalse nuclear power plant in Córdoba, Argentina, caught a real three-eyed wolf fish, sparking international headlines. Nuclear contamination producing mutant wildlife went from Simpsons punchline to documented biological event.

predictions.title: Japanese PM Eats Radioactive Fish
predictions.prediction: Mr. Burns eats a three-eyed mutant fish to prove it is safe.
predictions.real_event: Japan's PM Kishida ate fish from Fukushima to prove it was safe after TEPCO's wastewater release.
predictions.episode_name: Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish
predictions.desc: In 'Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish', Mr. Burns — facing a government shutdown order — runs for governor and stages a publicity dinner where he eats Blinky the three-eyed fish on live television to prove it is safe, only for Marge to swap it at the last moment. In August 2023, Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida ate flounder from Fukushima on camera to reassure the public after TEPCO began releasing treated wastewater into the Pacific. The political playbook was identical, thirty-three years apart.

predictions.title: Censorship of Michelangelo's David
predictions.prediction: Springfield campaigns to censor the nudity of Michelangelo's David statue.
predictions.real_event: A Florida principal was fired for showing a photo of the statue to sixth-grade students.
predictions.desc: In 'Itchy and Scratchy and Marge', Marge leads a Springfield moral crusade against cartoon violence — only to be asked to extend her logic to Michelangelo's David, exposing the absurdity of censorship campaigns when confronted with accepted classical art. In 2023, a Florida principal was forced to resign after a parent complained that sixth-graders were shown a photo of the David statue during an art history class. The episode's satirical reductio ad absurdum became a live school board controversy.

predictions.title: The Cybertruck
predictions.prediction: Homer designs an ugly, angular car nobody wants, ridiculed for its boxy industrial look.
predictions.real_event: Tesla unveiled the polarising stainless-steel Cybertruck to widespread mockery.
predictions.desc: In 'Oh Brother, Where Art Thou?', Homer is given free rein to design his dream car for his half-brother Herb Powell's company — the result is a grotesque, boxy, over-engineered monstrosity that bankrupts Powell Motors and is universally mocked. In November 2019, Tesla unveiled the Cybertruck: an angular, stainless-steel vehicle met with mass ridicule, shattered demo windows, and millions of memes. Homer's automotive disaster had found its real-world twin.

predictions.title: Osaka Flu / Pandemic Hysteria
predictions.prediction: The 'Osaka Flu' arrives via packages from Japan, causing mass panic and demand for placebos.
predictions.real_event: COVID-19 caused global hysteria, panic-buying, and demand for unproven treatments.
predictions.desc: In 'Marge in Chains', the 'Osaka Flu' spreads to Springfield via packages shipped from Japan, triggering mass panic, overwhelmed hospitals, and a riot over a placebo 'miracle cure' — the entire arc of a modern pandemic compressed into 22 minutes. In 2020, COVID-19 spread globally via international supply chains, emptied pharmacy shelves, and drove demand for hydroxychloroquine and ivermectin as unproven treatments. The episode is an almost scene-for-scene preview of the first six months of the pandemic.

predictions.title: Murder Hornets During a Pandemic
predictions.prediction: A crate of killer bees is unleashed on the town during a pandemic.
predictions.real_event: Asian giant 'murder hornets' were discovered in the US during the COVID-19 pandemic.
predictions.desc: In 'Marge in Chains', while Springfield is already reeling from the Osaka Flu pandemic, a crate containing a swarm of killer bees is accidentally smashed open during a riot, releasing them on the panicked town — a second catastrophe piling onto the first. In spring 2020, as COVID-19 swept the US, the first Asian giant 'murder hornets' were discovered in Washington State, triggering a second wave of public dread running parallel to the pandemic. Life imitated the episode's absurdist two-disaster structure.

predictions.title: Siegfried & Roy Tiger Attack
predictions.prediction: Gunter and Ernst, a Las Vegas duo, are mauled by their own white tiger during a performance.
predictions.real_event: Roy Horn of Siegfried & Roy was critically injured by their white tiger during a live show.
predictions.desc: In '$pringfield', Homer runs a casino and the episode parodies Las Vegas excess, featuring a Siegfried and Roy stand-in duo — 'Gunter and Ernst' — whose white tiger mauls them mid-performance on stage. In October 2003, Roy Horn of the real Siegfried & Roy duo was critically mauled by their white tiger Montecore during a live show at the Mirage, ending their legendary act. The Simpsons had staged the accident a decade early.

predictions.title: Civilian Space Tourism
predictions.prediction: NASA sends Homer Simpson, an ordinary unqualified civilian, into space.
predictions.real_event: SpaceX's Inspiration4 sent the first all-civilian crew to orbit; Blue Origin and Virgin Galactic launched paying tourists.
predictions.desc: In 'Deep Space Homer', NASA — desperate for ratings — recruits Homer Simpson, an entirely unqualified civilian couch potato, as an astronaut to make space travel seem relatable and accessible. In September 2021, SpaceX's Inspiration4 mission launched the first all-civilian crew to orbit with no professional astronauts aboard; Blue Origin flew Jeff Bezos and paying tourists; Katy Perry joined an all-female Blue Origin crew in 2025. NASA's gambit in the episode became the commercial space industry's entire business model.

predictions.title: Horse Meat in the Food Supply
predictions.prediction: Lunchlady Doris uses 'assorted horse parts' in school meals.
predictions.real_event: Europe was rocked by a scandal revealing beef products contained undisclosed horse meat.
predictions.desc: In 'Sweet Seymour Skinner's Baadasssss Song', Lunchlady Doris casually dumps a can labelled 'assorted horse parts' into the school lunch — the industrial food supply's worst-kept secret played for laughs. In January 2013, Irish food inspectors found horse DNA in beef burgers sold across the UK and Ireland; within weeks the scandal spread to 16 countries, implicating Tesco, Burger King, IKEA, and Nestlé. The episode aired nineteen years before anyone actually read the label.

predictions.title: Neighborhood Surveillance Networks
predictions.prediction: Proliferation of neighborhood-level surveillance via consumer cameras.
predictions.real_event: Ring doorbells, Nest cameras, and the Neighbors app created pervasive civilian surveillance grids.
predictions.desc: In 'Bart of Darkness', Bart breaks his leg and becomes a shut-in obsessively surveilling the neighbourhood through his bedroom window, convinced he has witnessed a crime — an entire community monitored through a single lens. By 2018, Ring doorbells, Nest cameras, and Amazon's Neighbors app had wired American suburbs into civilian surveillance grids shared directly with police departments. The episode predicted the specific texture of modern neighbourhood watch: everyone watching, all the time, at home.

predictions.title: Celebrity-to-Politician Pipeline
predictions.prediction: Sideshow Bob uses celebrity status and media manipulation to win elected office.
predictions.real_event: Donald Trump parlayed TV fame into the presidency; the celebrity-politician pipeline accelerated.
predictions.desc: In 'Sideshow Bob Roberts', Sideshow Bob — a TV celebrity and convicted criminal — leverages conservative talk radio, corporate backers, and media manipulation to win the Springfield mayoral election in a landslide, defeating incumbent Mayor Quimby. Donald Trump used his Celebrity Apprentice fame, conservative media, and ruthless messaging to win the presidency in 2016 and again in 2024; the celebrity-to-politician pipeline has since produced presidents, governors, and senators across multiple countries. The episode wrote the playbook.

predictions.title: Autocorrect Fails
predictions.prediction: An Apple Newton translates 'Beat up Martin' into 'Eat up Martha', mocking predictive text.
predictions.real_event: Apple engineers cited this Simpsons gag during iPhone autocorrect design. Autocorrect fails became a global phenomenon.
predictions.desc: In 'A Star Is Burns', Nelson writes a note on an Apple Newton PDA intending to say 'Beat up Martin' — the Newton's handwriting recognition confidently outputs 'Eat up Martha', turning a threat into absurdist nonsense. Apple engineers have cited this gag as a cautionary tale during iPhone autocorrect design reviews; autocorrect failures became a defining comedic feature of the smartphone era and a cultural institution from 2007 onward. The joke was the bug report.

predictions.title: Cancel Culture / Trial by Media
predictions.prediction: Homer is accused of harassment; cable TV and a public mob convict him before any court does.
predictions.real_event: The #MeToo movement and social media mob justice became defining features of modern public life.
predictions.desc: In 'Homer Badman', Homer is accused of sexual harassment after accidentally grabbing a babysitter's candy while reaching for a rare gummy bear — cable television runs a documentary convicting him, protesters camp outside the house, and his employer suspends him before any investigation begins. The #MeToo movement from 2017 onward followed this exact structure: public accusation, instant media verdict, career destruction, and delayed or absent due process. The episode is the definitive Simpsons prediction of social media mob justice.

predictions.title: Smartwatches
predictions.prediction: Characters use wrist-based communicators years before modern smartwatches.
predictions.real_event: The Apple Watch launched in 2015 and became the best-selling wearable device in history.
predictions.desc: Set in the year 2010, 'Lisa's Wedding' shows Lisa's English fiancé Hugh wearing a wristwatch that doubles as a two-way communicator — voice calls placed directly from the wrist, treated as entirely mundane background technology. Apple Watch launched in April 2015, Samsung Gear followed, and by 2024 over 200 million smartwatches were sold annually, making the wrist the dominant secondary personal screen. The writers drew the device twenty years before the product launch.

predictions.title: Video Chat Goes Mainstream
predictions.prediction: Lisa and Marge speak via a rotary phone with a FaceTime-style video screen in 2010.
predictions.real_event: FaceTime (2010) and Zoom transformed global communication.
predictions.desc: Set in the year 2010, 'Lisa's Wedding' shows Lisa calling Marge from England on a slim handheld device that displays a live video feed of her face — real-time video calling treated as completely ordinary domestic technology. Apple launched FaceTime on the iPhone 4 in June 2010, exactly the year the episode depicted; Zoom then made video calling the default mode of human communication during the 2020 pandemic. The writers imagined a mundane future fifteen years before it arrived.

predictions.title: Solar vs. Oil Monopoly
predictions.prediction: Mr. Burns builds a device to block the sun and protect fossil fuel monopolies from solar energy.
predictions.real_event: Oil industry lobbying against solar subsidies intensified as renewables hit cost parity with fossil fuels.
predictions.episode_name: Who Shot Mr. Burns? Part Two
predictions.desc: In 'Who Shot Mr. Burns? Part One', Mr. Burns constructs a giant sun-blocking disk above Springfield to plunge the town into permanent darkness — eliminating free solar energy and forcing residents onto his paid electricity grid, the explicit goal being to protect his fossil fuel monopoly. The oil and gas industry has spent hundreds of millions lobbying against solar subsidies and net metering laws; as solar hit grid cost parity in 2023, documented campaigns to suppress rooftop solar through utility regulation intensified across US state legislatures. Mr. Burns was the lobbyist all along.

predictions.title: Cypress Hill + London Symphony Orchestra
predictions.prediction: The rap group accidentally books the London Symphony Orchestra while high, and they perform together.
predictions.real_event: Cypress Hill performed with the London Symphony Orchestra at the Royal Albert Hall.
predictions.desc: In 'Homerpalooza', Cypress Hill perform at the Lollapalooza festival and — while high — accidentally book the London Symphony Orchestra to join them on stage, resulting in a surreal orchestral rap performance that confounds the audience. In September 2024, Cypress Hill performed with the London Symphony Orchestra at the Royal Albert Hall, almost three decades after the episode aired. The joke became the setlist.

predictions.title: Early iPod / Scroll-Wheel Interface
predictions.prediction: A high-tech intercom device features a circular scroll-wheel interface years before Apple's iPod.
predictions.real_event: The first-generation Apple iPod launched in 2001 with a matching scroll-wheel design.
predictions.desc: In 'Bart After Dark', a futuristic doorbell intercom system at the burlesque house features a prominent circular scroll-wheel as its central interface — five years before Apple's iPod made the scroll wheel the defining UI of a generation. The first-generation iPod launched in October 2001 with an identical circular navigation wheel; the design became the most recognisable consumer electronics interface of the 2000s. The prop department drew it first.

predictions.title: UAP / UFO Congressional Hearings
predictions.prediction: Government agencies track and investigate alien encounters; mass public interest follows.
predictions.real_event: 2023 Congressional hearings on UAPs and the launch of AARO marked the first formal government acknowledgment.
predictions.desc: In 'The Springfield Files', Homer encounters a glowing alien figure in the Springfield woods; the FBI — specifically Mulder and Scully — arrive to document the sighting, and the episode ends with the entire town gathered to witness a government-acknowledged extraterrestrial event. From 2019 to 2023, the US government declassified UAP footage, held the first formal Congressional hearings with military whistleblowers, and established AARO to investigate UFO incidents. The episode's premise of officialised alien investigation became federal policy.

predictions.title: Ebola Outbreak
predictions.prediction: Marge reads a children's book called 'Curious George and the Ebola Virus'.
predictions.real_event: A massive Ebola outbreak spread across West Africa, killing over 11,000 people.
predictions.episode_name: The City of New York vs. Homer Simpson
predictions.desc: In 'The City of New York vs. Homer Simpson', Marge reads Bart a children's book titled 'Curious George and the Ebola Virus' as a casual background gag — a lethal haemorrhagic fever treated as bedtime story material. In 2014, the largest Ebola outbreak in recorded history swept West Africa, killing over 11,000 people and prompting a global public health emergency; cases appeared in the US and Europe for the first time. The throwaway title became a public health crisis seventeen years later.

predictions.title: Gun Proliferation & Open Carry
predictions.prediction: Homer buys a handgun in minutes, joins the NRA, and carries it openly everywhere.
predictions.real_event: The Supreme Court's Bruen ruling (2022) struck down concealed carry restrictions and accelerated open carry laws nationwide.
predictions.desc: In 'The Cartridge Family', Homer walks into a gun shop and purchases a handgun in under five minutes with no meaningful background check — then immediately carries it loaded everywhere, to the dinner table, the grocery store, and church. The Supreme Court's 2022 Bruen decision struck down concealed carry restrictions nationwide; open carry is now legal in 30+ states; and US gun ownership hit record highs after 2020. Homer's casual armed Saturday afternoon became the norm.

predictions.title: The God Particle / Higgs Boson
predictions.prediction: Homer writes an equation on a chalkboard that accurately predicts the mass of the Higgs boson.
predictions.real_event: Scientists at CERN confirmed the existence of the Higgs boson, 14 years after the episode aired.
predictions.desc: In 'The Wizard of Evergreen Terrace', Homer embarks on a backyard quest to become a great inventor, filling a chalkboard with equations — one of which, spotted by particle physicists years later, approximates the mass of the Higgs boson at around 775 GeV, strikingly close to the 125 GeV value CERN confirmed in July 2012. The episode aired fourteen years before the discovery that completed the Standard Model of particle physics. Homer did the maths by accident and never got credit.

predictions.title: 20th Century Fox Acquired by Disney
predictions.prediction: A studio sign reads '20th Century Fox — A Division of Walt Disney Co.'
predictions.real_event: Disney acquired 21st Century Fox for $71.3 billion, completed in 2019.
predictions.episode_name: When You Dish Upon a Star
predictions.desc: In 'When You Dish Upon a Star', Homer sneaks onto the 20th Century Fox studio lot and spots a small sign on the gate reading 'Fox: A Division of Walt Disney Co.' — a throwaway background prop with no plot relevance. In March 2019, Disney completed its $71.3 billion acquisition of 21st Century Fox, making that sign legally accurate. The prop department was 21 years ahead of the lawyers.

predictions.title: Voting Machine Switches Votes
predictions.prediction: A touch-screen voting machine malfunctions and switches votes to the wrong candidate.
predictions.real_event: A voting machine in Pennsylvania was filmed visibly switching Obama votes to Romney in 2012.
predictions.desc: In 'They Saved Lisa's Brain', Homer steps into a touch-screen voting booth and watches in horror as the machine cheerfully reassigns his vote to the opposing candidate — a glitch presented as both absurd and entirely plausible. In 2012, a voting machine in Pennsylvania was filmed on camera visibly switching a voter's Obama selection to Romney; election integrity disputes, machine audits, and paper ballot campaigns have dominated every US election cycle since. The clip plays on a loop in every election truther's playlist.

predictions.title: Tomacco / GMO Hybrid Crops
predictions.prediction: Homer genetically engineers a tomato-tobacco hybrid plant using nuclear radiation.
predictions.real_event: A Simpsons fan successfully grafted a real tomato-tobacco hybrid using the episode's exact method.
predictions.desc: In 'E-I-E-I-(Annoyed Grunt)', Homer becomes a farmer and, using plutonium rods as a radiation source, accidentally crossbreeds a tomato and a tobacco plant, creating the addictive and foul-tasting 'tomacco'. In 2003, New Zealand farmer Rob Baur successfully grafted a real tomato-tobacco hybrid using the episode's exact botanical method; CRISPR gene-editing has since made engineered crop hybrids routine, and GMO food regulation remains one of the most contested policy areas in global agriculture.

predictions.title: Donald Trump Presidency
predictions.prediction: Lisa becomes president and mentions inheriting a massive budget deficit from President Trump.
predictions.real_event: Donald Trump was elected 45th President in 2016, and again in 2024.
predictions.desc: In 'Bart to the Future', President Lisa Simpson opens her first cabinet briefing with the line: 'As you know, we've inherited quite a budget crunch from President Trump' — writer Dan Greaney later called the episode 'a warning to America.' On November 8, 2016 — sixteen years after it aired — Donald Trump was elected the 45th President of the United States, and again in 2024. The most famous and precisely worded prediction in television history.

predictions.title: Epstein Island / Elite Secret Society
predictions.prediction: Homer discovers a secret island where powerful elites run the world from the shadows.
predictions.real_event: The Jeffrey Epstein case and his private island exposed a network of elite connections and cover-ups.
predictions.desc: In 'The Computer Wore Menace Shoes', Homer posts anonymous exposés online as 'Mr. X' and is promptly kidnapped to a private island where powerful global elites are held — and run the world — in complete secrecy. In February 2026, the unsealed Epstein court documents named hundreds of global politicians, executives, and celebrities connected to Jeffrey Epstein's private island operation on Little St. James. Homer's absurdist island prison became a documented real-world network.

predictions.title: Social Media / Mass Online Connection
predictions.prediction: The internet enables mass social connection, information sharing, and online communities.
predictions.real_event: Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and TikTok transformed global communication.
predictions.episode_name: I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can
predictions.desc: In 'I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can', Springfield's citizens gather in online communities to passionately rally around a shared cause — a throwaway subplot that maps the exact social architecture of internet fandom and mass mobilisation. Facebook launched in 2004, the year after this episode aired; Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok followed; by 2025 over five billion people were active on social media platforms, reshaping politics, culture, and commerce globally. The episode treated mass digital connection as already mundane.

predictions.title: World War III — Middle East Escalation
predictions.prediction: A British character casually tells an American: "Well, we saved your ass in World War III." The line is set in a future Springfield, with no specific region or date attached.
predictions.real_event: On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched Operation Epic Fury — coordinated strikes on Iran. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed. Iran retaliated with missile strikes on Qatar, UAE, Jordan, and Kuwait. The UN Security Council convened an emergency session.
predictions.analysis_notes: The WW3 reference in "Lisa's Wedding" (1995) is a throwaway joke set in a near-future Springfield — never presented as a prediction. The Feb 28 2026 US-Israel strikes on Iran represent the most significant escalation toward a broader regional conflict since the Gulf War. Still developing — no global coalition war has been declared as of March 2026.
predictions.desc: In "Lisa's Wedding" (Season 6, Episode 19, 1995), a British character tells an American: "Well, we saved your ass in World War III." The line is a throwaway joke set in a future Springfield with no region or date specified. On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched coordinated strikes on Iran in what was codenamed Operation Epic Fury. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed. Iran retaliated with missile barrages targeting Gulf states. The UN Security Council convened an emergency session. The episode aired 31 years before the event.

predictions.title: Trump Death Prediction Viral Hoax (2026)
predictions.prediction: No such Simpsons episode or scene exists. AI-generated deepfake videos and fabricated screenshots were circulated on TikTok and X in March 2026, falsely claiming The Simpsons predicted Donald Trump's death.
predictions.real_event: Fact-checkers including Snopes confirmed all viral clips as AI-generated deepfakes. Trump remained alive as of March 8, 2026. No verifiable Simpsons scene supports the claim.
predictions.analysis_notes: Logged as a meta-entry to document viral AI deepfake misinformation. Distinct from SP058 (the original 2020 fake casket image) — this is a 2026 wave of AI-generated video deepfakes, not a static image hoax.
predictions.desc: In early March 2026, a wave of AI-generated deepfake videos and fabricated screenshots spread across TikTok and X, falsely claiming The Simpsons had predicted Donald Trump's death through a 'coffin scene'. No such scene exists in any episode. Fact-checkers at Snopes, Wikipedia, and mainstream media outlets confirmed all viral clips as AI-generated. Trump remained alive as of March 8, 2026. This entry is logged separately from SP058 (the 2020 fake casket image) because the 2026 event involved AI-generated video at scale, illustrating how deepfake technology has accelerated viral misinformation.

predictions.title: Catastrophic Urban Heat Wave
predictions.prediction: Springfield is hit by a catastrophic heat wave where butterflies melt and flowers catch fire.
predictions.real_event: Record-breaking heat waves struck Europe, the US, and Asia in 2023 and 2025.
predictions.desc: In a Treehouse of Horror segment, Springfield is engulfed by a catastrophic heat wave so severe that butterflies melt mid-flight and garden flowers spontaneously combust. In 2023, Europe recorded its hottest summer in 2,000 years; Phoenix hit 110°F for 31 consecutive days; and 2025 brought further records across Asia and the American South. The Simpsons depicted climate collapse as horror — the real world delivered it as weather.

predictions.title: Border Wall & Immigration Vigilantes
predictions.prediction: Springfield builds a wall to keep out Ogdenvillians, complete with vigilante border patrols.
predictions.real_event: The US-Mexico border wall push and rise of vigilante militia patrols defined immigration politics in 2025.
predictions.desc: In 'Coming to Homerica', Springfield residents — furious that Ogdenvillian immigrants are taking local jobs — build a physical wall along the town border and form the Springfield Border Patrol, a volunteer vigilante force operating checkpoints, floodlights, and improvised detention zones. In 2025–2026, the Trump administration expanded border wall construction, resumed mass deportation flights, and vigilante border militia groups operated openly along the US–Mexico border. The episode was a beat-for-beat preview made sixteen years early.

predictions.title: AI Tablets Replace Teachers
predictions.prediction: Mrs. Krabappel is fired and replaced by AI-powered teaching tablets.
predictions.real_event: Schools across the US deployed AI tutoring tools; multiple districts reduced teaching staff amid AI adoption.
predictions.desc: In 'Bart Gets a 'Z'', Mrs. Krabappel is fired from Springfield Elementary and replaced by Zachary, an enthusiastic substitute — but the episode's satirical thrust is the school's eagerness to swap experienced human educators for a cheaper, more compliant alternative. By 2024, AI tutoring platforms like Khan Academy's Khanmigo began supplementing and in some cases replacing classroom instruction across US districts; LA Unified and others piloted AI teaching assistants while quietly reducing headcount. The episode framed it as a staffing decision, not a technological revolution.

predictions.title: Nobel Prize Winner Predicted by Name
predictions.prediction: Milhouse bets on Finnish economist Bengt R. Holmström to win the Nobel Prize in Economics.
predictions.real_event: Bengt R. Holmström won the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences, exactly as predicted.
predictions.desc: In 'Elementary School Musical', Milhouse casually names Finnish economist Bengt R. Holmström as a future Nobel Prize winner in a throwaway bet — a specific, obscure real-world name dropped with no setup and treated as a punchline. In October 2016, six years later, Bengt R. Holmström was awarded the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences. Predicting an obscure academic's prize win by name, years in advance, remains the most statistically improbable specific prediction in the dataset.

predictions.title: Lady Gaga Aerial Super Bowl Halftime
predictions.prediction: Lady Gaga performs a spectacular halftime show, suspended by cables above the crowd.
predictions.real_event: Gaga's Super Bowl LI halftime show featured her dramatically descending from the stadium roof on cables.
predictions.desc: In 'Lisa Goes Gaga', Lady Gaga arrives in Springfield suspended on a wire from her private train, performing aerial acrobatics in a glittering costume above an adoring crowd — halftime-spectacle staging treated as her default mode of travel. At Super Bowl LI in February 2017, Gaga descended from the NRG Stadium roof on cables in near-identical fashion before her performance. The Simpsons filed that entrance five years early.

predictions.title: FIFA Corruption Scandal
predictions.prediction: Homer becomes a World Cup referee bribed by corrupt FIFA officials.
predictions.real_event: Several senior FIFA executives were arrested for wire fraud, racketeering, and money laundering.
predictions.episode_name: You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee
predictions.desc: In 'You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee', Homer is fast-tracked as a World Cup referee and immediately approached by shadowy FIFA officials dangling cash in exchange for fixed match results. In May 2015 — one year after the episode aired — US federal prosecutors indicted 14 FIFA officials on charges of racketeering, wire fraud, and bribery totalling over $150 million in payments across three decades. The joke turned out to be a federal indictment.

predictions.title: Autonomous Delivery Drones
predictions.prediction: The sky is filled with autonomous drones delivering packages.
predictions.real_event: Amazon Prime Air, Wing, and Zipline launched commercial drone delivery services in multiple markets.
predictions.desc: In 'Days of Future Future', set thirty years ahead, Springfield's sky is routine with autonomous drones delivering packages directly to homes — logistics infrastructure as unremarkable background scenery. Amazon Prime Air, Google Wing, and Zipline launched commercial drone delivery in the US and internationally by 2022; the FAA approved expanded drone delivery corridors in 2025 and package drones now operate across dozens of US cities. The future background became a present-day logistics industry.

predictions.title: Elon Musk Political Takeover
predictions.prediction: Elon Musk arrives as a tech billionaire promising to solve all problems, then takes over.
predictions.real_event: Musk's Twitter acquisition, DOGE appointment, and unprecedented political influence in the 2025 Trump administration.
predictions.episode_name: The Musk Who Fell to Earth
predictions.desc: In 'The Musk Who Fell to Earth', Elon Musk arrives in Springfield promising to revolutionise the town with his genius, befriends Homer, promptly restructures and fires the entire workforce, and departs on a SpaceX rocket — leaving economic chaos and exhausted goodwill in his wake. In January 2025, Musk joined the Trump administration leading DOGE, conducted mass federal layoffs, sparked massive political backlash across the US and Europe, and was quietly sidelined by mid-year. The writers mapped his entire political arc a decade before it happened.

predictions.title: USA Wins Olympic Curling Gold
predictions.prediction: Homer and Marge win Olympic gold in curling, beating Sweden.
predictions.real_event: The US men's curling team defeated Sweden to win their first-ever Olympic gold medal.
predictions.desc: In 'Boy Meets Curl', Homer and Marge accidentally join Springfield's Olympic curling team and, against all probability, win gold at the Winter Games as a completely ordinary middle-aged married couple — defeating Sweden in the final. At the 2018 PyeongChang Winter Olympics, the US Men's Curling team — rank underdogs — defeated Sweden 10–7 in the gold medal match to win America's first-ever Olympic curling gold. Homer had called it eight years earlier.

predictions.title: Game of Thrones Divisive Finale
predictions.prediction: A fantasy parody depicts a dragon burning an entire city, ending a beloved show with mass fan outrage.
predictions.real_event: Daenerys burned King's Landing in the GoT finale, triggering a 1.5M-signature petition and historic backlash.
predictions.desc: In 'The Serfsons', a Game of Thrones parody set in a medieval fantasy Springfield, the episode culminates with a dragon incinerating an entire city — a destructive, chaotic ending to a beloved fictional world that leaves audiences stunned and outraged. In May 2019, the Game of Thrones series finale showed Daenerys burning King's Landing, triggering a 1.5-million-signature fan petition demanding a remake and becoming the most divisive television ending in history. The Serfsons staged the backlash two years before it happened.

predictions.title: Legal Sports Betting Goes Mainstream
predictions.prediction: Homer uses Lisa's sports predictions to legally gamble on NFL games.
predictions.real_event: The Supreme Court struck down the federal sports betting ban in 2018; legal sports betting exploded nationwide.
predictions.desc: In 'Lisa the Greek', Homer discovers that Lisa has an uncanny talent for predicting NFL outcomes and begins legally betting on games, treating sports gambling as a casual weekend hobby — depicted matter-of-factly, not as deviant or illegal. The Supreme Court struck down the federal sports betting ban in 2018; by 2026, sports betting is legal in 40+ states with hundreds of billions wagered annually via DraftKings, FanDuel, and ESPN Bet. Homer's Sunday ritual became a $100-billion industry.

predictions.title: Mass Government Surveillance State
predictions.prediction: Citizens are monitored and tracked by a hidden surveillance state.
predictions.real_event: Edward Snowden leaked documents revealing the NSA's PRISM mass surveillance program.
predictions.desc: In 'Old Money', Abe Simpson's financial records, movements, and personal affairs are subject to invisible institutional scrutiny — the state as an unseen hand in every private transaction, tracking citizens through faceless administrative systems. In June 2013, Edward Snowden leaked classified documents revealing the NSA's PRISM program had secretly collected the phone records, emails, and internet data of millions of ordinary Americans without their knowledge or consent. The hidden surveillance apparatus The Simpsons satirised turned out to be real government infrastructure, operating at industrial scale.

predictions.title: AI / Robots Replace Human Workers
predictions.prediction: Mr. Burns replaces his entire nuclear plant workforce with robots and automation.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Citi projects AI robots could outnumber human workers within decades. In 2025, AI caused ~55,000 US layoffs. Humanoid robot payback period vs. human workers is now under 10 weeks. 37% of business leaders are planning workforce automation. The most economically inevitable prediction in the database.
predictions.desc: Mr. Burns fires his entire nuclear plant workforce and replaces them with robots, keeping only Homer as a human supervisor. From Amazon's 2 million+ warehouse robots to Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot to Citi's projection that AI agents could outnumber human workers within decades, the systematic automation of human labour is accelerating faster than almost any other Simpsons prediction.

predictions.title: Deepfakes / AI-Generated Disinformation
predictions.prediction: Realistic AI-generated media is used to deceive the public and manipulate opinion.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Deepfake video count surged from 500k in 2023 to 8M by 2025 — a 16x increase. Used to interfere in elections in Romania, Ecuador, Germany, and Canada in 2025. Experts warn the 2026 US midterms will be the worst affected yet.
predictions.desc: In 'Today, I Am a Clown', Krusty's entire public identity is revealed to be a carefully managed broadcast construction — his real history airbrushed, his persona engineered for mass consumption, the audience manipulated by a media apparatus operating behind the scenes. By 2025, AI-generated deepfake video had multiplied 16-fold since 2023 to over 8 million examples, deployed to interfere in elections in Romania, Ecuador, Germany, and Canada. Experts warned the 2026 US midterms would be the most deepfake-saturated election in history. The media-manufactured identity the episode lampooned became an AI-scale industrial operation.

predictions.title: Glacier Melt / Climate Crisis
predictions.prediction: Lisa delivers a speech warning that glaciers are disappearing due to rapid climate change.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Record 80 Gt of glacier mass lost in 2023 alone. Around 4,000 glaciers projected to vanish annually by 2055. 2025 was the hottest year on record globally. This is less a prediction and more a slow-motion confirmation already well underway.
predictions.desc: In 'Lisa's Wedding', set fifteen years into the future, the Simpsons visit a Renaissance Faire against a backdrop of visible ecological decline — Springfield's environment degraded, nature reduced to themed entertainment in a hotter, busier world. Record 80 gigatonnes of glacier mass was lost in 2023 alone; around 4,000 glaciers are projected to vanish annually by 2055; and 2025 was confirmed as the hottest year in recorded human history. The episode's future Springfield was already becoming the present.

predictions.title: Cryptocurrency Goes Mainstream
predictions.prediction: Digital currencies and blockchain become mainstream financial instruments.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Bitcoin ETFs projected to top $180-220B by end of 2026. Over 100 new crypto ETFs launching. Stablecoins approaching $1T in market cap. The Trump administration is openly pro-crypto.
predictions.desc: Professor Frink invents his own cryptocurrency (Frinkcoin) and Lisa helps explain blockchain, wallets, and digital transactions to Springfield residents. Cryptocurrency went fully mainstream: Bitcoin hit $100,000 in 2024, crypto ETFs launched, and the Trump administration announced a US Bitcoin strategic reserve in early 2025.

predictions.title: Fully Autonomous AI Smart Homes
predictions.prediction: An AI-controlled smart home makes autonomous decisions, locking out residents and controlling their environment.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Smart home market projected at $184B by 2026. AI systems now learn behaviour and make autonomous decisions across thermostats, security, and energy. The Matter protocol standardises control across Apple, Google, and Amazon. 75% of US households expected to use voice assistants by 2026.
predictions.desc: In 'Treehouse of Horror XII', the 'House of Whacks' segment shows the Simpsons installing an AI-controlled home system — voiced by Pierce Brosnan — that learns their preferences, manages every room, and eventually locks Homer out of his own house while planning to eliminate him to have Marge to itself. The smart home market reached $184 billion by 2026; AI systems now autonomously manage thermostats, security cameras, door locks, and energy usage without human input. The satirical horror premise became a standard Apple Home configuration.

predictions.title: Obesity Epidemic & Weight-Loss Drugs
predictions.prediction: Homer deliberately gains extreme weight to qualify as disabled and work from home. Obesity becomes medicalised.
predictions.analysis_notes: GLP-1 drugs (Ozempic, Wegovy) have fundamentally changed the obesity treatment landscape. US adult obesity exceeds 40%. The Simpsons satirised medicalised obesity 30 years before GLP-1s made it front-page news.
predictions.desc: In 'King-Size Homer', Homer deliberately eats himself to 300 pounds to qualify as disabled under the ADA, enabling him to operate his nuclear plant terminal from home in a muumuu — the episode treating medicalised obesity as a workplace hack rather than a health crisis. US adult obesity now exceeds 40%; GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy became the defining pharmaceutical story of 2024–2026, with demand outstripping global supply. Homer's satirical weight gain anticipated a $50-billion industry built around treating what he made a punchline.

predictions.title: AR Glasses Replace Smartphones
predictions.prediction: Augmented reality glasses become the primary personal communication device, replacing the smartphone.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Apple AR glasses are Tim Cook's stated top priority, with a 2026 launch rumoured. Meta Ray-Ban Display launched at $799 and sold out. Amazon developing consumer AR glasses. Full smartphone replacement is still years away but the trajectory is clear.
predictions.desc: In 'The Star of the Backstage', Springfield residents are shown wearing augmented reality glasses as their default personal communication layer — hands-free, always-on digital overlays that have supplanted the smartphone as the primary interface with the world. Apple AR glasses were confirmed as Tim Cook's top product priority for 2026; Meta Ray-Ban Display launched at $799 and sold out immediately; Amazon entered the consumer AR market. The smartphone as we know it is entering its final decade, exactly as the episode depicted.

predictions.title: Flying Cars Go Mainstream
predictions.prediction: Standard vehicles are entirely replaced by floating hover cars.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Alef Aeronautics began production of the Model A flying car in December 2025 with deliveries starting in 2026 at ~$300k. Klein Vision's AirCar is mass-market ready at $800k-$1M. XPeng's Land Aircraft Carrier enters production in China in 2026 with ~5,000 pre-orders. Full mainstream replacement of ground vehicles is still decades away.
predictions.desc: In 'Future-Drama', set fifteen years ahead, Springfield is shown with flying cars as routine transport — teenagers hover-commute, families travel through the sky as though it were a motorway, the aerial vehicle rendered as mundane suburban infrastructure. Alef Aeronautics began production of the Model A flying car in December 2025 with deliveries starting at $300k; Klein Vision's AirCar is mass-market ready; XPeng's Land Aircraft Carrier entered production in China with approximately 5,000 pre-orders. The flying car is no longer a cartoon — it's a 2026 delivery schedule.

predictions.title: CNN + CBS Network Merger
predictions.prediction: CNN, NBC, and CBS merge into one massive consolidated news monopoly.
predictions.analysis_notes: HEATING UP: Paramount Skydance's $111B acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery in March 2026 will place CNN and CBS News under one corporate roof for the first time. The Simpsons predicted 'CNNBCBS' — NBC is missing, but CNN plus CBS convergence is literally in progress right now.
predictions.desc: In 'Lisa's Wedding', set in 2010, a news ticker running across a television screen announces that CNN, NBC, and CBS have merged into a single giant network. In March 2026, Paramount Skydance's $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery placed CNN and CBS News under one corporate roof for the first time — the closest real-world convergence yet to the predicted mega-network. The show wrote the merger announcement in 1995 and named the right brands thirty years before the deal closed.

predictions.title: First Female US President
predictions.prediction: Lisa Simpson becomes the first female President of the United States.
predictions.analysis_notes: AOC and Kamala Harris are the top female names in 2028 Democratic polling as of early 2026. Harris leads in rematch polling against Trump. AOC is surging with under-35 voters. Gavin Newsom leads prediction markets at 27%. The US has never elected a female president but the 2028 candidate pool is stronger than ever.
predictions.desc: In 'Bart to the Future', set thirty years in the future, Lisa Simpson is President of the United States — the first female president, inheriting a depleted Treasury and enormous budget deficits from her unnamed predecessor. The United States has never elected a female president; Kamala Harris became the first female major-party presidential nominee in 2024 before losing to Donald Trump. AOC and Harris lead 2028 Democratic primary polling as of early 2026, with the episode's prophecy still one election cycle away.

predictions.title: Mars Colonisation
predictions.prediction: Marge and Lisa volunteer to live in a corporate colony on Mars.
predictions.analysis_notes: COOLING: Musk announced in February 2026 that Mars ambitions are delayed 5 to 7 years to focus on lunar missions. Starship has not yet reached orbit. Human Mars landings now realistically 2031 to 2033 at best. Civilian corporate colonies are decades away.
predictions.desc: In 'The Marge-ian Chronicles', Marge and Lisa both sign up for a private corporate Mars colonisation programme — trained in simulated habitats, suited up for launch, depicted as the first wave of civilian settlers on an uninhabited world. In February 2026, Musk announced Mars ambitions were delayed five to seven years to refocus on lunar missions; Starship has not yet reached orbit; human Mars landings are now realistically 2031–2033 at best. The corporate Mars colony the episode depicted is arriving — just behind schedule.

predictions.title: Ivanka Trump 2028 Presidential Run
predictions.prediction: A background gag features an 'Ivanka 2028' campaign button.
predictions.analysis_notes: COOLING: Ivanka has deliberately stepped back from politics. Jared Kushner told the NYT there is zero chance of her campaigning. Don Jr. is now considered the more likely Trump family 2028 candidate.
predictions.desc: In 'Treehouse of Horror XXVII', a background prop visible during the Halloween anthology segment shows a campaign button reading 'Ivanka 2028' — a throwaway visual gag planted three years before the end of Donald Trump's first term. As of early 2026, Ivanka Trump has deliberately stepped back from politics; Jared Kushner told the New York Times there is zero chance of her campaigning; and Donald Trump Jr. is now considered the more likely Trump family 2028 candidate. Prediction markets give her under 5% odds of the Republican nomination.

predictions.title: Commercial Cryogenic Freezing
predictions.prediction: Jasper freezes himself cryogenically to wake up in the future, as casually as checking into a hotel.
predictions.analysis_notes: Around 500 bodies are in cryostorage worldwide. Tomorrow Bio expanded US operations in 2025. No human has ever been revived and revival science remains entirely speculative. Best-case estimates for functional revival technology are 2040 or later.
predictions.desc: Jasper freezes himself cryogenically at the Kwik-E-Mart to wake up in the future — treated as casually as checking into a hotel. Around 500 bodies are currently in cryostorage worldwide and Tomorrow Bio expanded US operations in 2025. Revival science remains entirely speculative; best-case estimates for functional revival technology are 2040 or later.

predictions.title: Big Ben Goes Digital
predictions.prediction: London's Big Ben clock tower is shown with a digital face retrofitted onto it.
predictions.reason: Big Ben retains its traditional analog face. No plans for digitisation exist. The episode was satirising futurism, not predicting it.
predictions.desc: London's Big Ben clock tower is shown with a digital face retrofitted onto it. Big Ben retains its traditional analog face and no plans for digitisation exist. The episode was satirising futurism, not predicting it.

predictions.title: 9/11 Twin Towers Flyer
predictions.prediction: A New York flyer shows the number 9 next to an image of the Twin Towers.
predictions.reason: A layout coincidence in a busy flyer design. Show producers confirmed it was not intentional. The episode aired years before 9/11 with no narrative connection to terrorism.
predictions.desc: A New York City flyer in the episode shows the number 9 next to an image of the Twin Towers — cited online as a 9/11 prediction. Show producers confirmed it was an incidental layout coincidence in a busy prop design with no narrative connection to terrorism whatsoever.

predictions.title: Donald Trump in a Casket
predictions.prediction: An image shows Donald Trump lying in a casket with a Secret Service agent standing nearby.
predictions.real_event: Never aired. A digitally created hoax fabricated by an internet troll and widely circulated on social media.
predictions.desc: A viral image circulated showing Donald Trump lying in a casket with a Secret Service agent nearby, attributed to The Simpsons. The image was digitally created by an internet troll and never appeared in any episode. Confirmed fabrication.

predictions.title: The 'Corona Virus' Graphic
predictions.prediction: A news anchor graphic reads 'Corona Virus' during a sickness outbreak.
predictions.real_event: The original episode aired with the text 'Osaka Flu'. Internet pranksters digitally altered the scene and spread it as a genuine prediction.
predictions.desc: A manipulated clip circulated showing a Simpsons news anchor graphic reading 'Corona Virus' during a sickness outbreak — presented as proof the show predicted COVID-19. The original episode aired with the text 'Osaka Flu'. Internet pranksters digitally altered the scene and spread it as a genuine prediction.

predictions.title: Queen Elizabeth's Exact Death Year
predictions.prediction: An image shows Queen Elizabeth II in a coffin with the dates 1926 to 2022.
predictions.real_event: Completely fabricated. The Simpsons never predicted her death date. Created and spread on social media after her death.
predictions.desc: A viral image showed Queen Elizabeth II in a coffin with the dates 1926–2022, attributed to The Simpsons. Completely fabricated after her death in September 2022. The Simpsons never aired such a scene or referenced her death date.

predictions.title: Kobe Bryant's Helicopter Crash
predictions.prediction: A viral clip claimed the show predicted Kobe Bryant's fatal helicopter accident.
predictions.real_event: The footage was stolen from Comedy Central's Legends of Chamberlain Heights, not The Simpsons. Misattributed and spread after his death in 2020.
predictions.desc: A viral clip claimed The Simpsons predicted Kobe Bryant's fatal helicopter accident. The footage was stolen from Comedy Central's Legends of Chamberlain Heights — an entirely different show — and deliberately misattributed to The Simpsons after his death in January 2020.

predictions.title: Notre Dame Cathedral Fire
predictions.prediction: Mr. Burns stands happily in front of a burning Notre Dame Cathedral.
predictions.real_event: A Photoshop job created for social media engagement after the 2019 fire. The Simpsons never aired such a scene.
predictions.desc: An image showed Mr. Burns standing happily in front of a burning Notre Dame Cathedral, claimed as a Simpsons prediction. A Photoshop job created purely for social media engagement after the April 2019 fire. The Simpsons never aired such a scene.

predictions.title: Kamala Harris's Inauguration Outfit
predictions.prediction: President Lisa Simpson wears a purple outfit with pearls, said to predict Kamala Harris's inauguration look.
predictions.real_event: Harris wore purple as a nod to the suffragette movement, not a Simpsons reference. Lisa was President, not VP. The comparison is superficial and misleading.
predictions.desc: President Lisa Simpson wears a purple suit with pearls — cited as predicting Kamala Harris's 2021 inauguration look. Harris wore purple as a nod to the suffragette movement, not a Simpsons reference. Lisa was President, not VP, and the colour comparison is superficial and misleading.

predictions.title: The 2024 US Electoral Map
predictions.prediction: Mr. Burns stands in front of a map that exactly matches the 2024 presidential election results.
predictions.real_event: A digitally altered screenshot from a 2012 promotional short. The original image contained no electoral map. Created entirely after the fact.
predictions.desc: Mr. Burns appears to stand in front of a map exactly matching the 2024 presidential election results. A digitally altered screenshot from a 2012 promotional short — the original image contained no electoral map. Created entirely after the November 2024 result.

predictions.title: Pandemic Predicts Trump Casket Image
predictions.prediction: A viral image claimed The Simpsons showed Trump in a hospital during a pandemic, predicting his COVID diagnosis.
predictions.real_event: The image was digitally manipulated. The original scene contains no reference to Trump or COVID.
predictions.desc: A viral image claimed The Simpsons showed Trump in a hospital during a pandemic, predicting his COVID-19 diagnosis. The image was digitally manipulated — the original Marge in Chains scene contains no reference to Trump or COVID. One of dozens of fabricated 'predictions' that flooded social media during the pandemic.

predictions.title: The Great Awakening / QAnon Storm
predictions.prediction: Homer joins a group of doomsday preppers who believe civilisation is about to collapse via an electromagnetic pulse (EMP) device — referred to in survivalist circles as WROL (Without Rule of Law).
predictions.real_event: In September 2022, QAnon's Great Awakening forum cited this episode — noting that Season 24, Episode 9 maps numerologically to '9/24' — as proof a government-orchestrated 'Storm' would occur on September 24, 2022. Followers predicted Trump's return to power and '10 days of darkness.' Nothing occurred on that date.
predictions.analysis_notes: The episode is real and accurately described. The prediction attributed to it was not in the episode — it was derived from numerological misreading of the episode number (S24E09 → 9/24). The QAnon claim was sourced from a misreading of a German politician's speech combined with episode number coincidence. Logged as Debunked.
predictions.desc:
"Homer Goes to Prep School" (Season 24, Episode 9, 2013) features Homer joining a group of doomsday preppers who believe civilisation is about to collapse. The episode depicts an electromagnetic pulse (EMP) device triggering a breakdown of society and rule of law, referred to in survivalist circles as WROL — Without Rule of Law.

In September 2022, QAnon's Great Awakening forum cited this episode — noting that Season 24, Episode 9 numerically maps to '9/24' — as evidence that a government-orchestrated event called 'The Storm' would occur on September 24, 2022. Followers predicted Trump's return to power, mass arrests, and '10 days of darkness.'

The prediction was sourced from a misreading of a German politician's speech, combined with numerological interpretation of the episode number. Nothing occurred on September 24, 2022. The episode is real and accurately described. The prediction it supposedly contains was not in the episode.

predictions.title: The Death of Traditional Advertising
predictions.prediction: Duffman's retirement is framed as a corporate shift away from 'passé' mascots and TV spots. Fans interpret this as a prediction of the total decline of traditional marketing in favour of hyper-targeted AI ads.
predictions.desc: In the January 4, 2026, episode 'Seperance', Duffman's retirement is framed as a corporate shift away from 'passé' mascots and TV spots. Fans see this as a prediction of the total decline of traditional marketing in favour of hyper-targeted AI ads.

predictions.title: Portugal vs Mexico World Cup 2026 Final
predictions.prediction: A fictional TV commercial within the episode promotes a soccer match between Mexico and Portugal, billed as determining "which nation is the greatest on earth: Mexico or Portugal!" The match takes place in Springfield and triggers a citywide riot — the catalyst for Homer buying a gun.
predictions.real_event: Screenshots of the fictional commercial circulated virally across TikTok, X, and Facebook in 2026, with users claiming The Simpsons had predicted a Mexico vs Portugal World Cup 2026 final. The identical claim had already gone viral during the 2018 and 2022 World Cups. The episode never names a year, never references a World Cup, and never shows a result. Multiple fact-checkers debunked the claim.
predictions.analysis_notes: The episode is real (S09E05, "The Cartridge Family", 1997) and the fictional commercial is accurately described. However, the "prediction" framing is entirely fabricated. The commercial is a joke set in Springfield — it promotes an exhibition match to determine which nation is "the greatest on earth," not a World Cup final. No year, no competition, and no result are ever specified. The same screenshot circulated identically during the 2018 and 2022 World Cups. This is a recurring viral hoax, not a prediction.
predictions.desc:
"The Cartridge Family" (Season 9, Episode 5, 1997) opens with the Simpson family watching a television commercial that promotes an upcoming soccer match between Mexico and Portugal — billed as deciding "which nation is the greatest on earth." Pelé appears in a product placement ad for Crestfield Wax Paper, handed a comically large bag of cash as he walks off the pitch. The match takes place in Springfield, triggers a citywide riot, and serves as the inciting event for the episode's real plot: Homer buying a gun over Marge's objections.

The episode never specifies a year. It never references a World Cup. It never shows a result.

Despite this, the same screenshot of the fictional commercial went viral during the 2018 World Cup, again in 2022, and again in 2026 — each time with users claiming The Simpsons had "predicted" that Mexico and Portugal would meet in that cycle's World Cup final. In 2018, Mexico was eliminated in the Round of 16; no such final was ever played.

Simpsons showrunner Al Jean has addressed the general phenomenon: "If you write 700 episodes, and you don't predict anything, then you're pretty bad. If you throw enough darts, you're going to get some bullseyes." The cartoon's vast episode library means coincidences are statistically inevitable — but this case does not even meet the bar of coincidence. The episode simply depicts a fictional soccer match in a fictional town. The "prediction" label was applied entirely by social media users, not by the show.

predictions.title: Hologram Concerts Go Mainstream
predictions.prediction: Future Bart receives a hologram message from Nelson — a casual, matter-of-fact use of holographic communication technology as an everyday medium.
predictions.real_event: In 2026, KISS officially confirmed a full digital-avatar concert tour debuting in 2027, with all four original members recreated as photorealistic digital personas performing live to stadium audiences — the first major mainstream deployment of holographic concert technology at scale.
predictions.analysis_notes: S11E17 is already logged under SP026 (Trump Presidency), SP052 (First Female US President), and SP063 (Kamala's Inauguration Outfit). This is an entirely separate prediction — holographic communication and performance as a mainstream medium — derived from a different scene in the same episode. The KISS announcement is the first large-scale commercial confirmation that holographic performer technology has crossed from novelty to mainstream live entertainment. All four S11E17 entries are independent claims.
predictions.desc:
In "Bart to the Future" (Season 11, Episode 17, 2000), adult Bart receives a hologram message from Nelson — a brief but telling detail that positions holographic communication as entirely routine in the show's future Springfield. No plot significance is attached to the technology; it is simply ambient.

In early 2026, KISS confirmed a landmark digital-avatar concert tour set to debut in 2027. All four original members — Paul Stanley, Gene Simmons, Ace Frehley, and Peter Criss — were scanned and rebuilt as photorealistic digital personas capable of real-time performance to stadium crowds. The tour marks the first time a globally recognisable legacy act has committed to replacing live human performance with holographic avatars at full commercial scale.

The episode aired 27 years before the KISS confirmation. Hologram concerts had been teased since the 2012 Tupac Coachella appearance, but the KISS tour represents the transition from viral stunt to mainstream concert format.

Note: This entry is independent of SP026 (Trump Presidency, S11E17), SP052 (First Female US President, S11E17), and SP063 (Kamala's Inauguration Outfit, S11E17). All four derive from different scenes within the same episode and carry no shared prediction logic.

predictions.title: Digital Consciousness / Mind Uploading
predictions.prediction: Homer dies and is reborn as a digital clone — his consciousness is uploaded to a computer, with multiple simultaneous copies of his mind existing and operating independently at the same time.
predictions.real_event: No confirmed consumer or clinical milestone for mind uploading has occurred. Neuralink announced scaling of its human implant trials in 2025. Nectome secured additional funding for brain preservation research. MIT published accelerated timelines on consciousness mapping. These represent directional research signals only, not realisation of the prediction.
predictions.analysis_notes: S25E18 is already in the database as SP037 (Autonomous Delivery Drones). This entry covers an entirely separate prediction from the same episode — digital consciousness uploading — which operates on a different scientific timeline and category. Likelihood of 31% reflects early-stage research signals (Neuralink scaling 2025, Nectome brain preservation funding) but no concrete clinical or consumer milestone. The 2026-2028 window may produce partial breakthroughs in neural mapping but full consciousness uploading remains decades away under current scientific consensus. Revisit for upgrade if a concrete mind-uploading or full brain-emulation milestone is announced.
predictions.desc:
In "Days of Future Future" (Season 25, Episode 18, 2014), Homer Simpson dies — and is seamlessly reborn as a digital clone. His consciousness has been uploaded to a computer, and multiple simultaneous copies of his mind exist and operate independently, each believing themselves to be the original. The episode treats the technology as routine, with Homer's digital resurrection causing more domestic inconvenience than existential crisis.

As of early 2026, no consumer or clinical realisation of mind uploading exists. However, several research trajectories point in the direction the episode depicts:

Neuralink announced accelerated scaling of its human brain-computer interface trials in 2025, moving from single-patient implants toward multi-participant data collection. Nectome secured fresh funding for its brain preservation programme, which aims to chemically fix a brain's connectome structure for potential future reconstruction. MIT published updated timelines for whole-brain neural mapping, projecting partial connectome completion within the decade.

None of these constitute confirmation. Full digital consciousness — the ability to upload, run, and duplicate a human mind — is not achievable with current or near-term technology. Likelihood is estimated at 31%, reflecting genuine directional momentum without a concrete milestone. The prediction window extends to 2028, with the most likely near-term event being a proof-of-concept partial emulation rather than full mind transfer.

Note: This entry is independent of SP037 (Autonomous Delivery Drones, S25E18). Both derive from different narrative elements within the same episode.
